title: Executive Summary
content:
NVDA closes 2026-01-25 with $216B of revenue (65.5% YoY) and $120B of net income (64.7% YoY). The period ended with an operating margin of 60.4% and a net margin of 55.6%, which frames how much of the reported scale translated into retained profitability. Revenue growth outpaced margin retention, so earnings improved without the same degree of operating efficiency.

Operating cash flow reached $103B (60.3% YoY), and after $6.04B of capital spending the business produced $96.7B of free cash flow (58.9% YoY). Capital spending absorbed 2.8% of revenue, which helps frame how much internal cash flow remained available after reinvestment.

Liquidity and leverage are both relatively manageable, with a current ratio of 3.91x and debt-to-equity of 0.06x. The quick ratio of 3.24x sits below the current ratio, indicating that inventory or other less-liquid current assets carry part of the coverage burden.

title: Key Metrics Dashboard
content:
*Snapshot period: FY2026 (Jan). Prior period: FY2025 (Jan).*

The dashboard follows the fixed metric contract; unavailable cells remain explicit instead of being hidden by issuer-specific row logic.
*Asset Turnover: Asset turnover uses average assets for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*
*Book Value Per Share: Book Value Per Share uses period-end shares outstanding.*
*Earnings Per Share (Diluted): Earnings Per Share (Diluted) uses diluted weighted-average shares from the income statement.*
*Return on Assets: ROA uses average assets for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*
*Return on Equity: ROE uses average equity for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*

title: Trend Analysis
content:
Free_cash_flow is the clearest multi-period driver in the locked annual history, with the latest value at $96.7B and a 85.7% compound annual growth rate. The scale-up across the observed annual periods is pronounced and financially material. The main trend relationship is therefore one of scale improving faster than margin retention.

The scale-up across the observed annual periods is pronounced and financially material. Cash generation remained constructive, with $103B of operating cash flow translating into $96.7B of free cash flow. Balance-sheet pressure should be read through both leverage (0.06x debt-to-equity) and liquidity (3.91x current ratio), because the two are not pointing to the same conclusion in every issuer.

title: Risk Factors
content: The most important risks in the current numbers are concentrated in the areas where profitability, cash generation, or balance-sheet support are not moving together. Current period is missing Marketable Securities while prior exists.

title: Analyst Notes
content:
The most constructive part of NVDA's profile is that gross margin of 71.1% reflects significant value capture, consistent with asset-light or ip-intensive business models. ROE of 101.5% is well above the 15% institutional threshold, suggesting strong capital allocation. That matters because leverage (0.06x debt-to-equity), liquidity (3.91x current ratio), and free cash flow ($96.7B) need to be read together rather than in isolation.

Analysis is anchored to the annual period ending 2026-01-25, with the prior period set at 2025-01-26. Metrics without the required statement support remain intentionally blank rather than estimated, so the report should be read as a filing-backed analytical note rather than as a gap-filled model.
